actions: Read external/README.md (full 283 lines) — xce_get_context described ONLY in steering rules (lines 113,130,144,158,171,180) as 'first step that combines search, architecture context, and tracing into one call'; absent from Available Tools section (lines 190-227) which lists the 4 single-purpose tools only

Read budget-allocator.ts (full 134 lines) — total budget 4000 tokens (line 54), codeGraph floor 1200 tokens (line 34), overflow redistribution priority constitutional→codeGraph→cocoIndex→triggered (line 41)

Read handlers/context.ts (full 416 lines) — single queryMode dispatch (lines 264-266), default budgetTokens 1200 (line 343), no multi-mode aggregation; extensive readiness gate (lines 182-262), seed normalization (lines 268-333), CocoIndex telemetry passthrough (lines 275-305)
Read iteration-001.md F-005 — proposed HLD/LLD schema available as reusable sub-mode for omni orchestration
Read iteration-002.md F-012 — trace pipeline uses queryEdgesTo(CONTAINS) + resolveSubjectFilePath; reusable as omni sub-mode
Read iteration-003.md F-019 — impact analysis reuses detect_changes output + queryEdgesTo/From + queryFileDegrees; reusable as omni sub-mode
Read code-graph-context.ts lines 32-55 — ContextResult interface has singular queryMode: no sub-results array, no multi-mode aggregation
summary: RQ4: FOLD omni capability into existing code_graph_context via new queryMode:'omni'. NEW tool option (380 LOC) duplicates readiness gates + seed normalization. FOLD-IN (117 LOC) reuses all existing plumbing. XCE's xce_get_context is steering-only (no dedicated I/O schema) — the omni mode matches its combiner semantics at 1/3 the LOC cost of a new tool. Internal sub-allocation: proportional budget split across 2-3 sub-mode buildContext() calls (40% neighborhood/30% trace/30% impact). No budget-allocator changes needed — caller budgetTokens override already supported.

label: XCE xce_get_context is steering-only combiner with no dedicated I/O schema in Available Tools section. Described in all 5 IDE steering variants as 'first step combining search, architecture context, and tracing into one call.'

evidence.quote: Call xce_get_context as your FIRST step when starting any task. It combines search, architecture context, and tracing into one call.
Call xce_get_context first on any task — it returns architecture, code locations, and relationships
Available Tools section lists xce_search, xce_architecture_context, xce_trace, xce_impact_analysis — xce_get_context is absent

label: Existing code_graph_context dispatches single queryMode per call with 1200-token default budget. No multi-mode aggregation. Adding queryMode:'omni' reuses all plumbing (readiness gate, seed normalization, fallback decisions, CocoIndex telemetry passthrough) at 117 LOC vs 380 LOC for a new tool.

evidence.quote: ContextResult: queryMode (singular), combinedSummary (singular), graphContext[] (per-anchor, not per-mode)

label: Budget-allocator has 4000-token total cap with 1200-token codeGraph floor. Overflow redistribution priority: constitutional→codeGraph→cocoIndex→triggered. Caller budgetTokens override already supported at context.ts:343 — omni mode can default to 2400 without touching budget-allocator.ts. Internal sub-allocation (40/30/30 split) lives in code-graph-context.ts.

evidence.quote: DEFAULT_FLOORS = { constitutional: 700, codeGraph: 1200, cocoIndex: 900, triggered: 400, overflow: 800 }
budgetTokens: args.budgetTokens ?? 1200 — caller override already supported, no allocator change needed

label: Omni internal design: 2-3 buildContext() calls with proportional budget split (40% neighborhood for architecture / 30% outline for trace / 30% impact for dependents). Concatenated output with section headers. Reuses RQ1 HLD/LLD, RQ2 trace, RQ3 impact analysis as sub-mode implementations.

evidence.quote: ContextResult provides per-anchor graphContext[], textBrief, combinedSummary — sub-mode results concatenated under section headers
queryMode validation: add 'omni' to the include set, branch to multi-call orchestration before single-mode dispatch
